rule_text: A trader should only enter trades when fundamentals, technicals, and market psychology are all aligned in the same direction, reducing trade frequency to ensure edge.

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch.0, p.6-165
raw_chunk_text:
But a lot of those people didn't keep their fortunes.

rule_text: A trader should concentrate trading capital in a single market where they have developed deep expertise and superior information, rather than diversifying across multiple markets with shallow knowledge.
entry_conditions: Trader has studied a single market for >= 24 months
Trader can articulate supply/demand fundamentals from primary sources
Trader has access to >= 3 expert advisors in the market
Trader has demonstrated edge with >55% win rate over 50+ trades in that market
exit_conditions: Win rate falls below 52% over rolling 30 trades
Information advantage erodes or expert sources become unavailable

Do you still talk to other traders about markets?
Not too much. Over the years, it has mostly cost me money. When I talk to other traders, I try to keep very
conscious of the idea that I have to liste
rule_text: A trader should validate that a trading idea is original and not already discounted by the market before committing capital, rather than acting on ideas adopted from other traders without independent conviction.
entry_conditions: Trade idea is self-generated (not sourced from other traders)
Trader has independently developed thesis for at least 5 trading days before entry
Estimated market participant adoption of this idea is below 10% of active traders
Position sizing reduced by 50% if any component of idea comes from external source
exit_conditions: Trade if market evidence suggests adoption rate exceeds 15%
Exit immediately if idea source is traced to another trader

rule_text: A trader should monitor consecutive directional days and sentiment indicators to evaluate market momentum and tone before initiating or holding positions, as these reveal shifts in underlying market structure that may invalidate the thesis supporting current positions.
entry_conditions: Position thesis based on fundamental catalyst or trend
Monitor consecutive up/down days each session
Track sentiment index percentile daily
exit_conditions: Exit 50% of position if 5+ consecutive days without sentiment confirmation
Exit remaining position if sentiment moves >20 percentile points opposite to price direction
Re-entry only after sentiment realigns with price direction

raw_chunk_text:
commodity markets were trading in lockstep fashion. On one particularly powerful day, almost all the markets went
limit-up. On that day, cotton opened limit-up, fell back, and finished only marginally
rule_text: Short a market that underperforms its peers when positive fundamental conditions should drive it higher, as relative weakness despite favorable news signals underlying weakness.
entry_conditions: Market is part of a correlated group (e.g., commodities, sector futures)
Peer markets are moving limit-up or showing strong directional strength (>2 standard deviations)
Target market lags peers by >3 days while positive sector news persists
Target market rebounds intraday but fails to close above opening
Confirm with volume: target market volume lower than peer average
exit_conditions: Market closes above 20-day moving average
Market shows 2 consecutive up days matching peer strength
Stop loss at highest intraday high since short entry + 2%

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. [Market Wizards introduction], pp. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
What kinds of misconceptions about the markets get people into trouble?
Well, I think the leading cause of financial disablement is the belief that you can rely on the experts to help
you. It might, i
rule_text: A trader must conduct independent research and analysis rather than relying on external experts or consensus opinion, because personal involvement and self-directed homework are essential prerequisites for profitable trading.
entry_conditions: Trader has completed independent technical analysis of price action
Trader has completed independent fundamental analysis relevant to the instrument
Entry signal aligns with own analysis (not based on broker/expert recommendation)
Confidence score from own research is >= 80%
exit_conditions: Exit when thesis from own analysis is invalidated
Exit at predetermined stop loss from own risk analysis

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch.0, p.6-165 (PSYCHOLOGY chunk)
raw_chunk_text:
Gut feel is very important. I don't know of any great professional trader that doesn't have it. Being a
successful trader also takes courage: the courage to try, the courage to fail, the courage to su
rule_text: A trader must cultivate intuitive decision-making ability (gut feel) while simultaneously maintaining the emotional discipline and courage to act on decisions through cycles of failure and success.
entry_conditions: Gut feel confidence score <= 6/10
Entry signal confirmed by at least 3 independent technical/fundamental factors
Trader has experienced and recovered from at least 1 significant drawdown in current market regime
exit_conditions: Exit at first sign of invalidation OR
Exit at 1% account risk maximum loss

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. [unnamed], pp. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
I really feel that if you can trade one market, you can trade them all. The! principles are the same. Trading is
emotion. It is mass psychology, greed, and fear. It is all the same in every situation.
rule_text: Early trading losses should not be interpreted as predictive of ultimate failure; persist through initial drawdowns as they are common among great traders, but use them as diagnostic signals to identify and correct systematic errors in your approach.
entry_conditions: Trading account equity drawdown < 10% from initial capital
Consecutive losing trades < 3 in a row
exit_conditions: Stop trading live and conduct diagnostic analysis when either threshold breached
Resume only after documented root cause identification and method adjustment

rule_text: A trader should enter a position in the direction of a breakout when a major fundamental change has occurred and the market has already begun to move in response to that change.
entry_conditions: Fundamental catalyst publicly announced or confirmed
Price breakout observed (above/below recent resistance/support)
Breakout has held for minimum 1-2 trading days
Trader feels uncomfortable with ambiguity (fundamental picture now clear)

parameters.entry_timing: Within first 2 days of breakout after catalyst

exit_conditions: Fundamental thesis disproven
Price closes below breakout level on high volume
Time stop after 15 trading days

rule_text: Follow the initial market direction when important fundamental developments occur, as informed traders often move first and the market's initial direction signals the longer-term trend.
entry_conditions: Important fundamental development is announced or occurs
Initial market directional move is established and sustained for at least 3 bars
Volume is above 75th percentile of recent average volume in the direction of the move
Price has moved at least 0.5% from the initial reaction low/high
exit_conditions: Fundamental thesis is invalidated by new information
Price closes below entry minus 2 ATR
Holding period exceeds 20 days without new confirmation

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. (excerpt pages 6-165), PSYCHOLOGY chunk
raw_chunk_text:
That's an interesting statement. What's right and what's black magic?
There is a great deal of hype attached to technical analysis by some technicians who claim that it predicts the
future. Technical
rule_text: A trader should use technical analysis as an information input (like a thermometer) to assess current market state, not to predict future prices, and integrate this assessment into trading decisions as a responsible market participant.
entry_conditions: Fundamental thesis established (analyst conviction exists)
Technical analysis confirms current market state aligns with thesis direction
Price action shows supportive pattern (moving average crossover, support hold)
exit_conditions: Fundamental thesis breaks OR
Technical confirmation signal reverses (below MA, support breaks)

rule_text: A trader should study price action and chart patterns to detect market disequilibria and regime changes before entering trades, as technical analysis reflects aggregate market behavior and unusual patterns signal actionable opportunities.

Volume or volatility regime shift observed alongside pattern

Market regime returns to stagnation (vol collapse)

rule_text: Trade breakouts from tight consolidation ranges when the breakout occurs without an obvious external catalyst, as these represent superior risk/reward opportunities compared to breakouts driven by news events.
entry_conditions: Price consolidating in range for minimum 20 bars
Range width <= 1.5% of consolidation midpoint
Volume during consolidation below 50th percentile of recent average
Breakout confirmed by close beyond range + 0.5% buffer
No major news/earnings/economic data released within 48 hours prior
Breakout volume >= 120% of consolidation average
exit_conditions: Stop loss at consolidation range low/high (opposite side of breakout)
Take profit at 3x risk target
Exit if reverses back into range on close

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. [untitled], pp. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
How about breakouts that occur because there is a story in the Wall Street Journal that day?
That would be much less relevant. The Heisenberg principle in physics provides an analogy for the markets.
rule_text: A trader should favor price breakouts that lack obvious external explanatory news, as unexplained moves are more likely to reflect genuine underlying shifts in supply/demand and sustain, while breakouts coinciding with media narratives are more likely to be distorted by observation/attention and fail to persist.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above consolidation high
No major news/earnings/economic data released in prior 5 trading days
Consolidation period ≥ 20 bars
Breakout volume > 50th percentile of trailing 20-bar average volume
Market regime is TRENDING
exit_conditions: Close back below consolidation high on 2 consecutive bars
Time stop: 10 bars without new high

rule_text: A trader should use systematic, non-mental stops (organized in advance) rather than discretionary mental stops to enforce exit discipline on losing positions, ensuring losses are contained before conviction in the trade idea can lead to compounding losses.
entry_conditions: Fundamental thesis identified and documented
Stop loss level calculated and locked in advance
exit_conditions: Exit automatically at stop loss level (no override)
If stopped out, require full fundamental re-analysis before re-entry

rule_text: A trader should reduce position size and correlation overlap when multiple positions move together, because highly correlated positions amplify hidden risk exposure beyond their nominal allocation.

exit_conditions: Close position if correlation to other positions rises above 0.70 on daily rebalance
Exit higher-correlation position when two correlated positions exist

rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades when price violently pierces a heavily-defended level with poor fills, as such breakouts are more reliable than typical breakouts despite worse execution.

exit_conditions: Exit on reversal below breakout level + 20 pips
Exit after 5% profit or 2% loss from entry

exit_conditions: Any of the 3+ diagnostic criteria are violated
Trader catches themselves using wish/hope language mid-trade (reduces position by 50%)

rule_text: A trader should enter a strong trend when other traders are forced to exit profitable positions prematurely due to emotional inability to hold gains, creating a contrarian entry opportunity with reduced competition.

Volume spike indicates forced liquidations/profit-taking
Entry on pullback within the trend, not at limit move
exit_conditions: Trend reversal confirmed (break of recent swing low/high)
Two consecutive closes against trend direction

Exit if position held >20 days without profit target

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Pages 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
And yet, all your rules are based on history. Is there a contradiction there?
No, because a good trend-following system will keep you in the market until there is evidence that the trend
has changed.
rule_text: A trader should use trend-following system logic to identify when a trend remains valid rather than imposing fixed historical boundaries on price movement, because markets can exceed historical extremes.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above recent swing high with increased volume
Moving average slope is positive (e.g., 20-period > 50-period)
exit_conditions: Close below 1.0x ATR from recent swing low
Price structure shows 2 consecutive lower lows without higher highs
20-period MA crosses below 50-period MA

raw_chunk_text:
After twenty years you get a bit punch-drunk.
Is there any advice you can give to other traders on how to stay emotionally calm during periods
of trading losses?
It is a little bit like playing golf:
rule_text: A trader should compartmentalize emotional responses to losses and avoid contaminating subsequent trading decisions with long-term macro scenarios or emotional state from prior trades.
entry_conditions: Prior trade has closed (win or loss)
Trader has completed a 15-minute reset period
New trade setup meets base statistical edge criteria only
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at initial risk level
Profit target reached
Time-based exit if no movement after 4 hours

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Paul Tudor Jones chapter, p.48-49
raw_chunk_text:
According to his own estimate, 95 percent of his profits have come from only 5 percent of his trades. Missing only a
few such profit opportunities could have a dramatic negative impact on performance.
rule_text: A trader should maintain disciplined focus on trading activity during losing periods, and avoid rigid market opinions that cause missed major trends, because the majority of profits concentrate in a small minority of trades.

Trading activity (review/analysis) frequency increases to daily
Market opinion conviction score drops below 0.7 to enable flexibility
Potential trend reversal signal present
exit_conditions: Exit losing trade if conviction score < 0.5
Equity recovery to within 5% of peak restores normal conviction thresholds

rule_text: A trader should enter in the direction of a range expansion in a previously narrow-range market, as the expansion signals the market is committed to moving in that direction.

entry_conditions: Consensus direction is established (>70% bullish/bearish in sentiment surveys or positioning data)
Price action contradicts consensus (e.g., yen declining while bullish dollar consensus dominates)
Trader has 2+ independent technical or fundamental signals supporting the contrarian view
Position size is ≤1% of account per trade
exit_conditions: Loss reaches 0.5% of account
Consensus reverses and price follows
Time-based exit after 10 trading days if thesis unconfirmed

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch.4, p.6-165
raw_chunk_text:
The point is that because people are the same, if you use sufficiently rigorous methods to avoid hindsight, you
can test a system and see how it would have done in the past and get a fairly good idea
rule_text: A trader should evaluate system performance using holding period analysis rather than arbitrary calendar-based periods, because human behavior (the true market driver) remains constant across time, allowing rigorous backtests to predict future performance with reasonable confidence.
entry_conditions: System backtest completed with rigorous methodology to avoid hindsight bias
System demonstrates positive expectancy across ALL holding periods from 1 to 252 days
Minimum 70% of holding periods show profitability
Holding period win rate consistent across multiple market regimes (trending, ranging, volatile)
exit_conditions: Exit when live performance deviates from backtest expectancy by more than 20% over 60 days of live trading

raw_chunk_text:
Essentially, our approach has three lights in determining the acceptance of trading signals. When the light is
green, we take all signals. When the light is yellow, we will liquidate an existing posit
rule_text: A trader should modulate position-taking aggressiveness based on market regime health, transitioning from full signal acceptance to liquidation-only to full liquidation as regime deteriorates, as a mechanism for rigid risk control.
entry_conditions: Regime light is GREEN: Take all valid trading signals
Regime light is YELLOW: Liquidate existing positions on signal; reject new entries
Regime light is RED: Liquidate all positions automatically; no new entries

parameters.green_to_yellow_threshold: 5% adverse move from regime entry OR volatility exceeds 20-day MA by 30%
parameters.yellow_to_red_threshold: 10% adverse move from regime entry OR volatility exceeds 20-day MA by 50%
parameters.regime_health_indicator: ADX < 20 (weak trend) triggers yellow; ADX < 15 triggers red

exit_conditions: On yellow light: Exit all new position attempts; close existing winners/breakevens
On red light: Liquidate all positions immediately regardless of P&L
On green light return: Resume normal signal-taking

green, we take all signals. When the light is yellow, we will liquidate an existing posit
rule_text: A trader should modulate position-taking aggressiveness based on market regime health, transitioning from full signal acceptance to liquidation-only to full liquidation as regime deteriorates, as a mechanism for rigid risk control.
entry_conditions: Regime light is GREEN: Take all valid trading signals
Regime light is YELLOW: Liquidate existing positions on signal; reject new entries
Regime light is RED: Liquidate all positions automatically; no new entries

parameters.green_to_yellow_threshold: 12% adverse move from regime entry OR volatility exceeds 20-day MA by 50%
parameters.yellow_to_red_threshold: 20% adverse move from regime entry OR volatility exceeds 20-day MA by 80%
parameters.regime_health_indicator: ADX < 15 triggers yellow; ADX < 10 triggers red
parameters.position_holding_duration_max: 4+ weeks; regime re-evaluation on signal failure only

exit_conditions: On yellow light: Hold existing positions; only reject marginal new signals
On red light: Liquidate all positions over 3-4 bars
On green light: Maximum position sizing allowed

raw_chunk_text:
I assume that if you trade all markets the same, you probably don't believe in optimization.
[Optimization refers to the process of testing many variations of a system for the past and then selecting
rule_text: Prioritize robustness and consistency across market conditions over curve-fitted perfection; avoid optimization-driven systems that perform well in backtests but lack forward-looking validity.
entry_conditions: Method has been tested on at least 5 uncorrelated markets
Win rate and profit factor are consistent (variance <15%) across all tested markets
No overbought/oversold indicator is used as primary entry signal
System parameters remain unchanged across all markets
exit_conditions: Sharpe ratio for any market drops below established baseline
Parameter drift detected requiring re-optimization

rule_text: When a market fails to respond to significant fundamental news in the expected direction, it signals a contrarian opportunity in the opposite direction.
entry_conditions: Major fundamental news event occurs (geopolitical, central bank, earnings, etc.)
News sentiment is clear and unambiguous (war = bullish for safe havens, rate cut = bullish for equities, etc.)
Market price movement is less than 20th percentile of typical responses to equivalent-magnitude news
Confirm weak response persists for at least 2 bars/periods
exit_conditions: Market reverses sharply in expected direction (>50% of initial weakness recovered)
Time stop: 5 trading days if reversal doesn't occur
News is contradicted or clarified with opposite interpretation

exit_conditions: Market moves in expected direction by 2x the initial underreaction amount

rule_text: A trader must size positions relative to account risk tolerance and never expose the full bankroll to a single trade, because losing all capital eliminates the ability to continue trading.

Maximum risk per trade ≤ 1% of account equity
Cumulative open drawdown ≤ 10% of account
exit_conditions: Exit at stop loss (defined risk)
Exit if cumulative drawdown reaches 10%

Economic data shows meaningful shift (PMI, unemployment, credit conditions change direction)
At least 2 of these 3 indicators aligned
exit_conditions: One of two confirming indicators reverses direction
Position drawdown exceeds 12% from entry
Economic thesis becomes ambiguous or contradicted

raw_chunk_text:
What are the elements of good trading?
Good trading is a peculiar balance between the conviction to follow your ideas and the flexibility to recognize
when you have made a mistake. You need to believe
rule_text: A trader should balance conviction in their thesis with intellectual humility and willingness to exit when evidence contradicts their original premise, while respecting counterparty information asymmetry.
entry_conditions: Trade idea has been formed with supporting thesis
Explicit pre-trade question asked: 'Why does the counterparty want the opposite side? What do they know?'
Risk/reward ratio is minimum 1:3 (provides margin for error)
exit_conditions: Price closes beyond 1.5 ATR against position
Failure to reach 25% of expected move within 3 bars
Unable to confidently answer: 'Why is counterparty on other side?'
New information emerges that contradicts thesis

rule_text: A trader should apply multiple independent filters combining fundamental and technical analysis to confirm contrarian signals, rather than relying on sentiment readings alone, to distinguish true reversal opportunities from false signals within extended trends.

Position entered only after first pullback confirmation post-signal

Exit if any filter reverses before target

So the first thing I learned about how to get superior performance is not to buy stocks that are near their
lows, but to buy stocks that are coming out of broad bases and beginning to make new highs r
rule_text: Enter positions in stocks breaking out from consolidation bases into new highs, rather than at depressed prices, to catch the beginning of major moves and avoid extended sideways periods.
entry_conditions: Price consolidates for at least 90+ days within a defined range (base formation)
Price breaks above the upper boundary of the base by at least 3%
Volume on breakout day exceeds 150% of 20-day average volume
Price closes above breakout level (not just intraday penetration)
exit_conditions: Close below the base upper boundary
Time-based exit after 180 days if target not reached
Trailing stop 8% below highest point since entry

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Chapter on CANSLIM, pp. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
Can you describe this model for picking winning stocks?
I use the easy-to-remember acronym CANSLIM. Each letter of this name represents one of the seven chief
characteristics of the all-time great win
rule_text: A trader should buy stocks only when current quarter earnings show strong year-over-year growth, as earnings momentum is a prerequisite for stock price appreciation.
entry_conditions: Current quarter EPS shows ≥70% YoY growth
Prior quarter also showed positive YoY EPS growth (>0%)
EPS growth is accelerating or stable (no deceleration)
exit_conditions: Current quarter EPS growth falls below 40% YoY
Two consecutive quarters of negative earnings surprise

rule_text: A trader should avoid stocks with excessive institutional ownership and instead seek stocks with moderate institutional backing, because over-owned stocks are prone to synchronized selling pressure and typically enter drawdowns after their outperformance becomes obvious.
entry_conditions: Stock institutional ownership between 30th-50th percentile of peer group
Meaningful institutional backing present (>10% ownership)
Stock moving in same direction as broad market averages
exit_conditions: Institutional ownership exceeds 65th percentile
Exit if broad market averages show topping patterns on daily price/volume analysis

rule_text: A trader should use a highly restrictive selection filter to ensure only the highest-quality candidates enter the portfolio, accepting that most trades will be modest winners but concentrating capital on the exceptional few.
entry_conditions: Candidate meets 95th percentile or higher across ALL selection criteria (e.g., CANSLIM composite score ≥95)
Candidate rank in universe ≤5th percentile by quality score
Portfolio universe size <200 eligible candidates per 1000 screened
exit_conditions: Position drops below entry quality threshold
Position reaches +50% target or decays below entry criteria

Wouldn't most of the indicators in your CANSLIM formula, such as EPS, pick up a stock before it
goes to new highs? Why not just buy the stock when it is still forming a base instead of waiting for it
rule_text: Enter a position at the breakout point from a consolidation pattern rather than anticipating the breakout, to minimize the probability of loss and avoid being shaken out by normal intra-pattern fluctuation.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above base resistance level
Relative Strength (RS) >= 85
Volume confirmation above 20-day average
Close remains above resistance at end of day
exit_conditions: Price closes below base resistance
Loss reaches 5% from entry

You have stated that the superior stocks have high relative strength figures—80 or higher.
Although high relative strength is good, is there such a thing as it being too high? In other words, might a
rule_text: A trader should buy stocks with high relative strength (≥80) only when they are emerging from a sound base-building period and have not yet extended more than a threshold percentage beyond their prior price base, avoiding overextended positions vulnerable to correction.
entry_conditions: Relative strength ≥ 80
Stock just emerging from sound base-building period (5-10 days post-breakout)
Current price ≤ prior price base + 5% extension
exit_conditions: Price closes below the base
Extension reaches 15%
Stop loss at base support level

Stock breaks toward lower 25% of prior base range

One element of your trading style is buying a stock when it makes a new high. Wouldn't the
fundamental screening conditions you use have been in place well before that point?
In some cases, they might
rule_text: Buy stocks at new highs to avoid overhead resistance from underwater shareholders, ensuring most market participants are profitable and unlikely to exit positions.
entry_conditions: Price closes above 20-week high (relative to 52-week base)
Volume > 100% of 20-day average volume
Fundamental screening conditions met
Stock consolidated for minimum 4 weeks before breakout
exit_conditions: Close below breakout price - 5%
Stop loss at 5% below entry

rule_text: Use volume surge as a filter to confirm breakout validity before entry, avoiding whipsaw trades on breakouts lacking conviction.
entry_conditions: Price breaks to new highs (above recent swing high)
Volume increases by at least 100% (doubles or more) vs. recent 20-day average
Close remains above breakout level
exit_conditions: Close below breakout level on lower volume
Price retraces 50% of breakout move

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch.0, p.96
raw_chunk_text:
96
So, in the consolidation phase, decreasing volume is good. If you continue to see very high
volume, do you start thinking potential top?
Yes, because that shows that a lot of people are getting out
rule_text: A trader should interpret volume behavior as a confirmation signal: decreasing volume during consolidation suggests strength, while increasing volume on price reversals (especially at support levels) indicates genuine buyer commitment.
entry_conditions: Price attempts new lower low but fails to break previous swing low
Volume on reversal bar exceeds 50th percentile of last 20-bar average
Close above open on reversal bar (bullish reversal candle)
Consolidation period preceded by at least 3 bars of decreasing volume
exit_conditions: Exit if price makes new low below failed support
Exit if volume dries up during upward move

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. (unspecified), p. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
buyers coming in.
The screens you have described in selecting stocks are essentially O'Neil's CANSLIM methodology.
[See the O'Neil chapter for definition.] Did you add any of your own elements to his
rule_text: A trader should favor stocks with valuations relative to the broad market index, specifically targeting lower P/E ratios within a bounded range of the S&P 500 P/E, because these exhibit higher success ratios than both absolute low P/E and high P/E stocks.
entry_conditions: Stock P/E ratio >= S&P 500 P/E ratio (no discount floor)
Stock P/E ratio <= 1.3x S&P 500 P/E ratio (tight ceiling)
Stock meets CANSLIM criteria
exit_conditions: Stock P/E exceeds 1.3x S&P 500 P/E ratio
Stock violates CANSLIM criteria

rule_text: A trader should initiate short positions when a stock exhibits deteriorating fundamental and technical characteristics: poor growth, decelerating earnings, loss of relative strength, broken uptrends, and new lows.
entry_conditions: 5-year growth rate < -10% (poor growth confirmed)
Quarterly earnings growth rate declining for >= 3 consecutive quarters
Relative strength rank < 20th percentile vs peers
Price breaks below 200-day moving average (broken uptrend)
Price makes new 52-week low
exit_conditions: Position stop-loss: 8% above entry
Earnings surprise positive (fundamental reversal)
Price closes above 200-day MA for 2 consecutive days

you can do in a bear market is just sit it out.
How do you recognize a bear market before it's too late?
By how well my individual stocks are doing. If, during the bull phase, the leaders start losing
rule_text: A trader should reduce or exit positions when leading stocks underperform during a bull phase or when multiple consecutive positions are stopped out, as these are early signals of regime change from bull to bear market.
entry_conditions: Currently in bull phase with long positions
Market leader or sector bellwether shows breakdown (close below prior support or moving average)
OR: 2+ consecutive individual positions stopped out within 10 trading days

parameters.leader_weakness_definition: Any close below 20-day MA or prior swing low

exit_conditions: Exit all long positions immediately upon signal trigger
Move to 100% cash or hedge with put spreads

rule_text: A trader should exit a long position when a rally following a significant decline shows weakness through low volume and declining breadth (fewer stocks participating), signaling deteriorating market internals despite price recovery.
entry_conditions: Market has declined significantly from recent high (>2% pullback)
A recovery rally has begun from the low
Volume on the recovery rally is below the 20-day average volume
Advance/decline line fails to reach the previous rally high by >5%
Fewer than 40% of stocks participate in the rally
exit_conditions: All four conditions confirmed on daily close
Exit full position on next open or intraday weakness

source_citation: Market Wizards, Jack Schwager, Ch. [pages 6-165], p. 6-165
raw_chunk_text:
What advice would you give the novice trader?
The single most important advice I can give anybody is: Learn from your mistakes. That is the only way to
become a successful trader.

Position size is capped at 8% account risk per trade
Intellectual bias acknowledged: thesis is viable despite potential biases
exit_conditions: Thesis is fundamentally broken
Account drawdown exceeds 15%
Position reaches 4x initial risk target

rule_text: A trader should attribute losses to controllable factors (inexperience, poor execution, mistakes) rather than external luck, and use this attribution to systematically eliminate the root cause before risking capital again.
entry_conditions: Any loss realized on a trade
Root-cause analysis completed identifying controllable error (inexperience, execution flaw, or preparation gap)
Documented corrective procedure(s) implemented (minimum 3 distinct changes)
Drawdown threshold: resume at 50% of pre-loss position size
exit_conditions: Resume original position sizing only after 20 consecutive profitable trades
If another loss occurs before 20-win streak, reset counter and reduce size again

Trade moves against expectation by 1.0% unrealized loss

Hold winning positions until 1.5:1 risk-reward to maximize trend participation
Accept larger losses in service of following the rule system

rule_text: Under stress, traders revert to simple, heuristic-based decisions and crowd-following behavior, which are reliably unprofitable. A trader should implement pre-planned, deliberate decision rules and avoid reactive pattern-matching during periods of elevated emotional stress.
entry_conditions: Trade setup meets pre-defined criteria (established before market entry)
Current unrealized P&L drawdown < 5% of account
No trades entered in last 2 hours (cooling-off period enforced)

parameters.crowd_following_filter: Skip if >60% of peer trades in same direction

exit_conditions: Exit at profit target or stop-loss established at entry (non-negotiable)
If emotional state elevated (manual flag), reduce size by 50% on next trade

rule_text: A trader should regulate their physical state (posture, breathing, muscle tension) to control their emotional state, which directly influences trading performance.
entry_conditions: A trade setup is identified
Trader performs pre-entry physical state check: assess posture alignment, breathing rate, muscle tension
If any physical indicator is suboptimal (shallow breathing, hunched posture, or detectable tension), trader pauses for 2 minutes to reset physical state
Only enter trade after physical reset confirmation
exit_conditions: Exit at original stop-loss or profit target
Additional exit if physical distress returns during trade; reduce position by 50%

raw_chunk_text:
• I'm too impatient with the markets.

• I'm afraid at the wrong time.
• I'm too optimistic about what will happen.

rule_text: A trader should actively monitor and correct their mental state through physical/physiological interventions (posture, breathing, muscle control) before or during trading when they detect identifiable emotional distortions (impatience, anger, fear, overoptimism).
entry_conditions: Pre-entry self-assessment detects any of: impatience, anger, excessive fear, or overoptimism
Trader performs 5-minute body posture correction + diaphragmatic breathing protocol
Emotional state re-assessment shows improvement
Only then: proceed with original trade thesis
exit_conditions: If reset protocol fails to improve mental state after 5 minutes, skip the trade entirely
Exit any open position immediately if emotional distortion recurs mid-trade

rule_text: A trader should weight option purchases and strategies based on time remaining until expiration and volatility estimates, because longer duration and higher volatility increase the probability of favorable intrinsic value moves and option value appreciation.
entry_conditions: Days to expiration >= 90 days
Realized or implied volatility >= 75th percentile of historical range
Position size = 1-2% of account per position
exit_conditions: Exit if days to expiration falls below 30 days
